observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a.) For the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned personnel (by issuing an
executive order) in ensuring the full implementation of the one-time cleansing
of PPE; and
b.) For the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Physical Inventory Plan
and ensure completion of the physical inventory-taking of all properties.
For the Municipal Accountant:
c.) Prepare, update, and maintain subsidiary records like the PPE ledger cards for
each item of property and equipment of the municipality; and
d.) Effect necessary adjusting entries and provision of necessary disclosure in
the notes to financial statements.
For the General Services Officer (GSO):
e.) To maintain property cards for each PPE item in order to facilitate
reconciliation and control;

We recommended the following to the management:
To Municipal Accountant:
a) Secure the monthly bank statement and perform a timely bank reconciliation to
identify and adjust journal entries for all valid reconciling items; and
b) Make the necessary adjusting entry for the unrecorded bank credit memo.
To Municipal Treasurer:
c) Assign/designate personnel in recording the daily transactions in the
Cashbooks for Cash in Bank, ruling and footing the cashbooks at the end of
each month, and conducting at least monthly reconciliation between the Cash
in Bank balances per cashbook and bank statement;
d) Ensure that the cashbooks are updated daily and conduct regular reconciliation
between cashbook balances and bank balances for all of the accounts (even in
instances that the unadjusted balances are the same) and monitor and
record/adjust any valid cashbook reconciling item in the Cashbooks for Cash
in Bank to reflect the correct cashbook balances of the respective Cash in Bank
accounts.
To Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
e) Facilitate the proper disposition or closure of the dormant bank accounts; and
f) Perform a periodic reconciliation of the accounts to avoid the accumulation of
unreconciled items in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement regular monitoring to track
transfers to the Special Trust Fund and conduct review of transfers to the Special
Trust Fund to identify any irregularities.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a) Strengthen the alignment of planning documents by ensuring that both the Annual
Budget and the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are prepared and approved within
the prescribed period of the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for
LGUs;
b) Sustain and institutionalize the effective utilization of the 20% development fund
by continuing to prioritize implementation-ready projects, programs, and activities
(PPAs). This ensures that the fund contributes meaningfully to socio-economic
and environmental outcomes, in accordance with the intent of DBM-DOF-DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020; and
c) Improve the timeliness of project implementation by addressing factors causing
delays and strengthening project execution strategies. This will help ensure that
PPAs funded under both current and continuing appropriations are completed
within their scheduled timelines for more effective delivery of public services.

We recommended that the Management optimize the utilization of its unexpended
LDRRMF from previous years transferred to special trust fund to lessen the socio-
economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster
risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and
response capabilities of the community.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a) Strictly enforce the No Segregation, No Collection policy at the household in
barangay levels, as well as in other waste sources such as markets, commercial
establishments, institutions, schools, hospitals, and industries; and
b) Ensure that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan is reviewed and updated
within the recommended period by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its
PPE;
b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in planning, execution, and reconciliation of GSO and
Accounting records;
c) Adhere to the necessary procedures in the conduct of proper disposal of the
unserviceable PPEs, review the accuracy of the dropped accounts, and effect
necessary adjustments, if warranted;
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards
and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation and conduct further investigation on the variances noted during the
actual count;
e) Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure of relevant information in
the Notes to Financial Statements for the subsequent years for appropriate
understanding;
f) Direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the
Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the
accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular
No. 2015-008 and submit the pertinent reports as prescribed by existing laws,
rules, and regulations; and
g) Give priority to the directives set under the COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts which will provide benefits to the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government depository
bank for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding
monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance
with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011;
and
b) Prepare an adjusting journal entry to recognize all the valid book reconciling
items and effect the necessary entries on the recording of the dormant bank
account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges and
formulate a more effective, efficient, sustainable, and responsive revenue
collection procedures and strategies; and
b) Tap additional sources of income as authorized under RA 7160 to increase locally
sourced revenues and lessen its dependence on the internal revenue allotments.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Ensure optimal utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services
and benefits to constituents; and
b) Ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20% DF that partake the nature of
capital expenditure shall be recorded in their proper accounts in the financial
statement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Implement regular monitoring to track transfers to the Special Trust Fund and
conduct review of transfers to the Special Trust Fund to identify any irregularities;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting journal entry to
transfer the unexpended balance of MF-MOOE from the general fund to the trust
fund as prescribed. Henceforth, transfer the yearly unutilized balance of DRRM Fund
at the end of the year to reflect the correct balance of the trust liability account at
every accounting period; and
c) For the Municipal Treasurer, to ensure that all disbursements paid by the LGU are
supported by acceptable evidence of receipt of payment.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for
the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team;
b) Ensure that transactions and disbursements charged to GAD funds are properly
supported with sufficient and competent documents;
c) Require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) of the Municipality to have a
continuous education on GAD related activities and to institutionalize the GAD
database with adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated
data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming,
and policy formulation; and
d) Require the GFPS to establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to come
up with a more GAD responsive and related projects, activities, and programs
(PAPs).

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Prioritize the establishment of well-designed and properly managed sanitary
landfills;
b) Ensure the sanitary landfill is operational as soon as possible for efficient and
effective management of solid waste; and
c) Secure funds for the immediate establishment of the sanitary landfill, consider
exploring various funding sources such as local government budget allocations,
grants from environmental agencies, public-private partnerships, and community
fundraising initiatives. Implementing waste management fees or environmental
taxes could also generate necessary funds. Additionally, the municipality could
apply for national or international environmental grants.

We recommended and the Management agreed to coordinate with the Water District,
DPWH, DENR-EMB, DOH, and other stakeholders in the locality to fast-track the
preparation and implementation of the Septage Management Plan in its area of
jurisdiction. This could include generating additional fund sources, possibly by
enacting ordinances to adjust local property taxes or imposing a service fee system
to meet the necessary expenses for its proper implementation.
Moreover, we recommended to ensure that the concerned employees attend regular
training and capacity-building programs to stay informed about best practices and
innovations in water management.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to the following courses of actions:
a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its
PPE;
b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in planning, execution, and reconciliation of GSO and
Accounting records;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards
and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of records and conduct further investigation into the circumstances
surrounding the loss of some items that were not found during the actual count;
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the depreciation thereof based on
the latest estimated useful life of assets and take-up corresponding prior years’
adjustment in the books to be closed to the Government Equity account, and
provide disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements for
appropriate understanding;
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts all
completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and
direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the
Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the
accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular
No. 2015-008 and submit the pertinent reports as prescribed by existing laws,
rules and regulations; and
f) Give priority to the directives set under the COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts which will provide benefits to the municipality.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
of the following courses of action:
To Municipal Accountant:
a) Secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government depository
bank for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding
monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance with
Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011;
b) Prepare an adjusting journal entry to recognize all the valid reconciling items and
effect the necessary entries on the recording of the dormant bank account;
To Municipal Treasurer:
c) Regularly monitor the bank accounts of the Municipal Government and
communicate immediately with the offices concerned about any changes in the
bank account status.
To Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:
d) Analyze the dormant bank account and communicate with the source agency to
determine the nature and terms of the account. If warranted, cause the closure of
the dormant account, and transfer the balance to the general fund to protect the
amount from possible risk of loss of rights and ownership.

Relative to the foregoing audit observation, we recommended and the management
agreed to:
a) Direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash
advance. Non-compliance will constitute a valid cause for the withholding of
his/her salary and the instruction of other sanctions as provided in COA Circular
No. 97-002;
b) Enforce strictly the provision of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and require the concerned
officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to immediately settle the
same; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the accountable
officers and provide a copy of the demand letters to the Audit Team as
confirmation of the Municipal Accountant's action to demand
liquidation/settlement.

With the foregoing audit observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide relevant information disclosures in
the Notes to Financial Statements for an appropriate understanding of the
municipality's actual financial status.

To further improve the reliability of financial reporting, we recommended and the
management agreed to:
a) Record and adjust the accounts affected in order to put the intra-agency accounts
in agreement considering the noted variances as of December 31, 2022;
b) Henceforth, simultaneously record transactions relating to the reciprocal
accounts; and
c) Ensure regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts for better and fair
presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges;
b) Come up with plans and strategies to increase their collections on real property
taxes;
c) Tap additional sources of income as authorized under RA 7160 to increase
revenues and avoid total dependence on the internal revenue allotments; and
d) Strictly implement the tax ordinances enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan relative
to revenue generation program.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Ensure that the 20% development fund be optimally utilized for socio-economic
and environmental outcomes pursuant to the intent of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, by prioritizing the
projects, programs and activities which are attainable/implementation-ready to
ensure efficient and effective fund governance;
b) Reinforce and/or adjust existing planning and budgeting practices and techniques
to further improve the orderliness of the Municipality’s processes to be able to
comply with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs;
c) Formulate realistic and attainable work plan by installing performance tracking
mechanisms to monitor the implementation of continuing projects and taking into
consideration the factors affecting performance identified in the monitoring
process; and
d) Reiterate to the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence and professional
care in reviewing disbursement vouchers to ensure the completeness and propriety
of the supporting documents upon submission.

c) Immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and their supporting

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed

a) Promote media literacy and improve information dissemination to assist

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed

a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for
the timely submission of the said reports to the COA;
b) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD

c) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-

adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will

formulation and establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to come up
with a more GAD responsive and related programs and activities.

b) Require the FDP Focal Person to be prompt and accurate on the posting of the

c) Maximize the utilization of the FDP Board and website of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following:
• Upon the completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the
Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete
and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD
144 and Section 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures
cited in COA Circular 89-296;
• Determine the recoverable amount from the obsolete and unserviceable
assets and recognize appropriate impairment loss;
• Immediately review the accounting records to determine the assets eligible
for dropping and reconcile with the records of the property custodian;
• Continuously reconcile the result of the physical count with the property
and accounting record;
• Provide depreciation on the assets of the Agency following the guidelines
provided under COA Circular No. 2004-003; and
• Adjust the understatement of Accumulated Depreciation in previous years
to be able to present the PPE at its proper valuation.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal
Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair
value whichever is applicable and provide the appropriate depreciation for the
depreciable component of the road network following the straight-line method
without provision for residual value;
Require the Municipal Accountant to recompute the accumulated
depreciation of the completed Road Network Assets and to prepare Journal
Entry Voucher recognizing correction or adjustments for the understatement
thereof; and
Further for the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant, the
MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform
their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road
Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of the ensuing year the required inventory
reports duly reconciled with the Accounting records particularly the Report on
Local Road Network (RLRN) and Report on the Physical Count of the Local
Road Network (RPCLRN).

• Enhance internal control of cash by formulating written internal
guidelines and procedures governing cash in banks to address the
identified deficiencies and gaps and ensuring that these guidelines are
fully disseminated to and understood, and implemented by the
concerned offices/employees of the municipal government.
• Improve transparency and accountability over public funds, ensure
compliance with laws, rules, and regulations regarding the timely
preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements,
including supporting documents such as, but not limited to Bank
Statements, SL of the Bank Accounts as well as other supporting
schedules.
• Register the bank accounts of the Municipality in the weAccess of its
AGDB for the online access of its statements.
• Revisit actual practices of the municipal government and align them
with applicable standards and related guidelines and procedures for the
proper disposition and treatment of transactions affecting cash in a
bank account.
• Prepare correcting entries for identified errors in Subsidiary Ledgers.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the
Management agreed to the following:
• Ensure that continuing appropriation balances pertain only to projects
and activities, such as those for the construction of physical structures
or for the acquisition of real property or equipment.
• Require the municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting journal entry
to transfer the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE from the
general fund to the trust fund as prescribed. Henceforth, transfer the
yearly unutilized balance of DRRMF at the end of the year to reflect the
correct balance of the trust liability account at every accounting period.
• The LDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP strictly in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-002 by incorporating the detailed plan for the
unexpended balance of the 30% Quick Response Fund (QRF) and 70%
MOOE of the LDRRMF for proper utilization and submit the Council’s
Resolution to the Sangguniang Bayan for authorization if there are
changes made to the items charged to the LDRRM Fund.
• The MDRRMO to submit written explanation for the reason of
disbursing/obligating the prior years’ unexpended balance without
inclusion in the revised LDRRMF and approval of the Sangguniang
Bayan. Moreover, refrain from utilizing the unexpended portion of
previous years’ DRRM fund without revision of Annual Investment Plan
and authority from Sanggunian.
• The Municipal Accountant and other officials/employees concerned to
ensure the completeness of data/information and other supporting
documents on the procurement and distribution of goods and services.

• Establish policies and procedures to ensure that current employees
receive enough continuous mentoring, and supervision in order to
effectively fulfill their tasks.
• For the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all Revenue Collectors to
turn-over the collections daily and intact. Moreover, require the Municipal
Treasurer to verify the Report of Collections submitted by all accountable
officers and deposit intact all the collections.
• Require the Accountable officers to reconcile their book balance with the
cash on hand daily, foot, balance, rule and close/certify the entries in the
cashbook at the end of every month.

Considering all the aforementioned conditions, we have recommended
and the management agreed to:
For the Local Chief Executive to:
• Require the offices/officials concerned to identify the gaps in the
determination and implementation of development projects thereby coming
up with corrective measures to ensure optimal utilization of funds and
timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to constituents.
For the Municipal Development Council and Engineering Office to:
• Assign specific timelines for the identified development projects to
evaluate achievement rate and identify areas with a significant shortfall or
needs improvement to make timely and periodic remedial measures and
determine the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented
projects.
• Prepare necessary documentation in the implementation of LDF projects,
such as but not limited to independent inspection reports.
For the Local Budget Officer to:
• Determine the status of excess funds form completed projects, ensure that
the aforementioned funds were free from any encumbrances, and
recommend to the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian Bayan for its
revision and re-appropriate it to other development projects of the LGU.
For the Municipal Accountant to:
• Exercise due diligence and professional care in the review of documentary
requirements to ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting
documents upon submission and ensure compliance with BIR rules and
regulations in withholding taxes.
• Ensure completed Development Infrastructure Projects were fully insured
against possible structural failures/defects through requiring contractors
in posting warranty security in the form and amounts prescribed in the
Revised IRR of RA 9184 before processing the final payments.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the
management agreed to the following:
o Establish an internal control structure that provide assurance that
transactions are recorded, reconciled, processed, and summarized to permit
accurate preparation of financial statements.
o Check, verify and reconstruct the statement of cash flows manually to
present an accurate and reliable data.
o Evaluate and revisit the electronic accounting system employed by the
LGU to avoid occurrence of the same error.

Considering all the aforementioned conditions, we have recommended
and the management agreed to:

o Immediately submit a copy of all unsubmitted contracts entered into by
the Municipal Government of Pikit together with all the documents
forming part thereof.
o Provide justification for the delayed submission of the contracts and
bidding documents.
o Prospectively, ensure timely submission of the contract documents in
the compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a) Require the General Services Officer to maintain updated Property Cards per
category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of Property,
Plant and Equipment of accounting records, and thereafter as the transaction
occurs;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards consistent
with their accounting records;
c) Require both offices to reconcile property records ensuring that the balances
shown in the prepared RPCPPE agreed with the supply records and the book
balances in the accounting records, and vice versa;
d) For any resulting difference, the Accounting and the General Services Office
should work out on tracing the reconciling items, establish its
existence/nonexistence and determine from there any further action that may be
undertaken;
e) Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every three
years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RPCPPE;
f) Ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without ARE and property tags
with complete information such as, tag inventory number, property number, serial
number of the property, description and other salient information; and
g) Prepare and submit reconciled copy of the RPCPPE to the COA Auditors on or
before the 31st day of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant
to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is
applicable and provide the appropriate depreciation for the depreciable component
of the road network following the straight-line method without provision for
residual value;
Require the Municipal Accountant to recompute the accumulated depreciation of the
completed Road Network Assets and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher recognizing
correction or adjustments for the understatement thereof; and
Further for the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, and the
Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and
responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated
under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit to the Auditor not later than January 31 of
the ensuing year the required inventory reports duly reconciled with the Accounting
records particularly the Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) and Report on the
Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the GSO adhere
strictly to the provisions of the GAM for LGUs on the proper recording and
reporting of transactions which requires the LGU to:
a) Book all regular purchases of supplies through the inventory account
whether it is consumed or not within the accounting period;
b) Maintain the Inventory Records using the Perpetual Inventory Method and
continually update it for every additions and subtractions from the
inventory;
c) Ensure the proper maintenance of subsidiary ledger cards for control and
monitoring purposes; and
d) Prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, supported with the
Requisition and Issue Slips, as well as the stock cards, which shall serve as basis
for recording the issuance of supplies and materials and the preparation of the
Journal Entry Voucher.

We recommended to the management the following:
(a) Regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts shall be conducted to
ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash
advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon
their nature and purpose; and
(b) For the management to have a credit risk policy in place, and the exposure to
credit risk is monitored on an on- going basis and establish an allowance for
impairment that represents its estimate of anticipated losses in respect of receivables.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled-up drivers’ trip
tickets to support the payment of gasoline amounting to 901, 531.60. Moreover, for
future transactions, the management has to strictly enforce complete and proper
accomplishment of drivers’ trip tickets to support payments of gasoline and oil to
minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to
prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section
5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Require the faithful adherence of the rules and regulations on BGCM Fund so as not to
defeat the purpose of the law as reiterated in Republic Act (RA) No. 11469 or the
Bayanihan to Heal as One Act for the emergency procurement.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to
prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of BGCM
Fund on a monthly basis, in compliance with paragraph 4 of LBC No. 125 dated
April 7, 2020.
Require the duly authorized personnel to post in the LGU’s official website and in at least
three (3) conspicuous places in the locality the accumulated reports of BGCM Fund at the
end of every month.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive Officer, as head of the agency to:
1. Require the Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain complete
property plant and equipment ledger cards as required under Sec. 43 of
NGAS, Volume I & Sec. C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property
Custodianship;
2. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct year-end physical inventory on
Property, Plant and Equipment, including Other Land Improvements, Other
Infrastructure Assets and some Buildings and Other Structures and submit it
to the Accounting for reconciliation of balances as a requirement to Sec. 57 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I to ensure correctness and reliability of the PPE
account balances;
3. Require the Accountant and the General Services Officer to analyze the
discrepancy between the balances in the books and per physical count of the
PPE Accounts and to make the necessary adjustments in their records to take
up the noted differences affecting current year’s transactions. Also, locate the
missing/unreconciled properties;
4. Require the GSO to annually provide a copy of the reconciled RCPPPE to the
COA, not later than the 31st day of January of ensuing year, showing therein
the proper classification of PPE a shown in PPSAS accounts, Also, prepare
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties. These
unserviceable properties may still have been included in the total PPEs subject
for reconciliation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the
depreciation of the abovementioned PPE accounts by a debit to depreciation expense or
debit to prior years’ adjustments for the current year’s depreciation and prior years’
depreciation, respectively and a credit to accumulated depreciation in accordance with the
guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation of depreciation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management create an inventory committee with fix
duration to conduct semestral physical inventory of supplies and materials and prepare
the corresponding RPCI duly reconciled with the property and accounting records and
submit to the Auditor within the prescribed dates.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record asset accounts upon receipt of
supplies and materials inventory/ies and recognize corresponding expense account upon
issuance/utilization thereof in accordance with government regulations.
We also recommended for the management to require the GSO to prepare and
maintain Stock Cards (SCs) for each type of supplies and materials and for the
Accounting to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for supplies and materials
acquired and issued to support the end year balances of the supplies and materials
inventory and expense accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is
applicable and provide the appropriate depreciation for the depreciable component of the
road network following the straight line method without provision for residual value;
Require the Municipal Accountant to recompute the accumulated depreciation of the
completed Road Network Assets and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher recognizing
correction or adjustments for the understatement thereof; and
Further for the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, and the
Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and
responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated
under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit to the Auditor not later than January 31 of
the ensuing year the required inventory reports duly reconciled with the Accounting
records particularly the Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) and Report on the
Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN).

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile
the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect
the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial
operation as of a given period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Section 89 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular 97-002. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to
accountable officers who have previous unsettled cash advances and
discontinue granting cash advances to elective officials for purposes other
than stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002, dated February 10, 1997.
b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or
satisfactory explanation is received within thirty (30) days, appropriate
remedies may be applied such as the withholding of salary due to the
accountable officer/s. Moreover, evaluate collectability of the long-standing
receivables and assess the possibility of write-off pursuant to the criteria set
forth by COA Circular No. 2016-005.
c. Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal actions against the
accountable officers for non-compliance with the above-cited regulations, if
warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to install appropriate control in the
maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers of all real accounts to ensure correctness
of the General Ledger Balances and will also serve as basis in remittance of
trust liabilities and/or payment of other obligations incurred by the
municipality;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that appropriate taxes are
withheld and remitted on time to the BIR after ensuring the correctness
thereof with complete documentation;
3. Adopt appropriate measure/s to be undertaken to settle the issue pertaining to the
double remittance of taxes withheld made to BIR under the General Fund;
4. Direct the proper inclusion of claims for tax refunds and/or credits in the Notes to
the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the General Services Officer to maintain updated Property
Cards per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in
the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records, and
thereafter as the transaction occurs;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards
consistent with their accounting records;
c. Require both offices to reconcile property records ensuring that the
balances shown in the prepared RPCPPE agreed with the supply records
and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa;
d. For any resulting difference, the Accounting and the General Services
Office should work out on tracing the reconciling items, establish its
existence/nonexistence and determine from there any further action that
may be undertaken;
e. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every
three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the
prepared RPCPPE.
f. Ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without ARE and
property tags with complete information such as, tag inventory number,
property number, serial number of the property, description and other
salient information.
g. Prepare and submit reconciled copy of the RPCPPE to the COA Auditors
on or before the 31st day of the ensuing year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the
depreciation of the abovementioned PPE accounts by a debit to depreciation
expense or debit to prior years’ adjustments for the current year’s depreciation and
prior years’ depreciation, respectively and a credit to accumulated depreciation in
accordance with the guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation
of depreciation.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under
Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.
b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or
satisfactory explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the
withholding of salary due to the accountable officer/s. Moreover,
evaluate collectability of the long-standing receivables and assess the
possibility of write-off pursuant to the criteria set forth by COA
Circular No. 2016-005.
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable
officers who have previous unsettled cash advances.
d. Institute appropriate administrative and/or legal actions against the
accountable officers for non-compliance with the above-cited
regulations, if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect
adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on
the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to immediately remit all taxes
withheld and adhere strictly with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012
to avoid imposition of penalties interest and surcharges.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to insure all properties,
owned by the municipality, to the GSIS as required under the above-cited
regulation to enable the municipality to recover the cost of properties in case of loss
or damage.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management must secure local sanggunian
authority/approval for all the contracts/projects entered into/implemented by the
local chief executive that were described in generic terms in the Appropriation
Ordinance/AIP for CY 2018 to ratify the same and avoid disallowances pursuant to
G.R. No. 175527 and COA Memorandum 2010-014.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General
Services Officer/Supply Officer to furnish COA copies of perfected
contracts/purchase orders and inspection reports within the prescribed period for
timely review, evaluation and inspection.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the BAC to invite in writing the Auditor and the two
(2) observers of private group and NGO in all stages of procurement at least five
calendar days before the activity in compliance to Section 13.1 and 13.3 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the members of the BAC should adhere strictly to the
provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184
and to fully use the PhilGEPS in the procurement process.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to require the Municipal Accountant
to coordinate with the concerned servicing banks for the timely submission of the
Bank Statement with supporting documents and to prepare/submit the BRS to the
Office of the Auditor within the required period for review. Necessary adjusting or
correcting entries must be prepared immediately by the Municipal Accountant for
proper presentation and reconciliation of bank accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain a separate
book of accounts for the Philhealth Capitation Fund for the receipt and utilization
of the same to facilitate timely preparation and submission of the required reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer & Municipal Planning &
Development Officer comply the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure
regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the year in proper
format.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the General Services Officer to maintain updated Property Cards per
category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of
Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records, and thereafter as the
transaction occurs;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards consistent
with their accounting records;
c. Require both offices to reconcile property records ensuring that the balances
shown in the prepared RPCPPE agreed with the supply records and the book
balances in the accounting records, and vice versa;
d. For any resulting difference, the Accounting and the General Services Office
should work out on tracing the reconciling items, establish its
existence/nonexistence and determine from there any further action that may be
undertaken;
e. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every three
years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared
RPCPPE.
f. Ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without ARE and property tags
with complete information such as, tag inventory number, property number, serial
number of the property, description and other salient information.
g. Prepare and submit reconciled copy of the RPCPPE to the COA Auditors on or
before the 30th day of the ensuing year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the
depreciation of the abovementioned PPE accounts by a debit to depreciation expense or
debit to prior years’ adjustments for the current year’s depreciation and prior years’
depreciation, respectively and a credit to accumulated depreciation in accordance with the
guidelines set by relevant COA regulations in the computation of depreciation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Members of the Municipal School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirements set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No.
10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various
activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with
the guidelines on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in
Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1. S.2017 of DepEd, DBM, and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers, and other stakeholders of the ECCD
System in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD
Programs.

Recommendation:
We recommended the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify
possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes.
This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria
mentioned in the said act.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile
the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect
the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial
operation as of a given period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the local chief executive to direct the Municipal Accountant
a) To issue Demand Letter to concerned official and employees notifying them of
their accountabilities and demanding immediate settlement thereof;
b) That in case of failure of liquidation/settlement, to cause the withholding of the
salary of the concerned officials and employees notwithstanding the imposition of
other sanctions provided for in COA Circular 97-002; and
c) To stop granting additional cash advances, unless previous cash advances have
been fully liquidated and settled.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Identify authorized Local projects that necessitate the hiring of job order
workers;
b) Align the hiring of job order personnel services with the Sanggunian authorized
local projects.
c) Review the approved Plantilla of the LGU & evaluate existence of unfilled
clerical positions that need to be filled up instead of Jos.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions
on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from the
personnel's payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted
loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the
collecting agents/office of these creditors.
Ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least P5,000.00 for CY
2018 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act for CY 2018

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management reinforce efforts on the collection of audit
disallowances as required under Sections 5.4 and 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and
necessary documentations on the actions of the Municipality must be compiled, copy
furnish the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer comply the provisions of Circular
No. 0213-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at the beginning of the
year in proper format as well as the quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the GSO adhere strictly to
the provisions of Section 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs on the proper recording and
reporting of transactions which requires that all regular purchases of supplies should be
booked through the inventory account whether it is consumed or not within the accounting
period and maintenance of subsidiary ledger cards. The GSO or each Department Head
should conduct an inventory to establish the existence of the office supplies, medical,
dental, and laboratory supplies and drugs, agricultural supplies, medicines and other
items/ inventories for stock to serve as basis for reconciliation the same with the reports of
the accounting. The GSO should also maintain stock cards for control and monitoring
purposes and submit the Summary of Supplies and Material Issued, supported with the
Requisition and Issuance Slips to the Municipal Accountant, which shall serve as basis for
recording the issuance of supplies and materials and the preparation of the Journal Entry
Voucher.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should maintain special accounts
for market and 20% development fund operations in accordance with the provisions of
Chapter 6, Volume I of NGAS so that management would be informed on the financial
position and condition of each special account as of a given time to guide them in taking
significant actions to boost the operation of each account.

identification of project under the AIP of the 20% Development Fund and its fund
utilization recording;

corresponding asset account/s in the financial statements; and
c. Prepare adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recording of public infrastructure
project under the 22% Development Fund amounting to P12,494, 321.60.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20% development fund shall partake the nature of capital expenditure and investment and record corresponding asset account/s in the financial statements.

We recommend the Management to appropriate and utilize 20% development fund to concrete and definite development projects/programs and ensure implementation and completion of projects/programs within the timeframe or as calendared.  Avoid reversion and/or transfer of funds from one project/program to another. The Management shall at all times adhere to the policy guideline set forth for fiscal administration with paramount consideration on delivery of basic services and benefits to the public.

We recommend the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

We recommend that the local chief executive to direct the Municipal Accountant
To issue Demand Letter to concerned official and employees notifying them of their accountabilities and demanding immediate settlement thereof;
That in case of failure of liquidation/settlement, to cause the withholding of the salary of the concerned officials and employees notwithstanding the imposition of other sanctions provided for in COA Circular 97-002; and
To stop granting additional cash advances, unless previous cash advances have been fully liquidated and settled.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency and enforce the collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P6,812,281.17.

We recommend that the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following:
The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking.  A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and
The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommend to the Management to establish one (1) public slaughterhouse in an area allowed under the zoning ordinance that will serve the need for a fast, efficient and sanitary slaughtering facility in the municipality.  Also, we recommend the Management to take every opportunity to improve economic condition of the municipality for the benefit of the inhabitants.
We recommend that the Municipality Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines.

We recommend that Management enjoin concerned officials and employees to prepare and submit immediately those unsubmitted basic financial reports.  We also recommend that the LCE direct regular submission of basic financial reports within due date from now on and sanction those erring officials and employees who fail to do so.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to prepare Contract of Service/Job Order stipulating the specific duties and responsibilities to be performed by the job order worker and other necessary provisions and conditions set forth in the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM)-Volume I, Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and other Personnel Actions, Rule XI, Local Government Code Republic Act 7160, and other pertinent rules and regulations as this should become the basis in the assessment and evaluation of performance for the re-hiring of the personnel.

Require the Job Orders/Contractual personnel to prepare and submit Accomplishment Reports, together with the duly accomplished Daily Time Record (DTR), in support to the Payroll claims for the services rendered for the period. 

Direct the HRMO to submit the Certification required in the COA Circular 2012-001stating that the activities/services cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the agency thus necessitating the need of hiring job order workers. 

Direct the HRMO, Accounting and other responsible offices to verify completeness and accuracy of documents prior to processing the transactions and submission thereof to COA Office. In relation to this, ensure receipts of payments are properly acknowledged by respective payees and documents forwarded to the office are original copies. 

Devise policies and guidelines in the hiring of job order workers in conformance to the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and other Personnel Actions, Rule XI, Local Government Code Republic Act 7160, COA rules and regulations as it this would form as a vital basis for the hiring of personnel under Job Order arrangements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Local Chief Executive should instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to prepare the payrolls while the Municipal Accountant should supervise the preparation of payrolls and ensure compliance to the aforementioned provisions of GAA. 

We further recommend that management discontinue the practice of deducting payments of monthly loan amortizations of private lending establishments from the personnel’s salary and wages payroll. Those personnel whom have loan agreement with private lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/s or office of respective lending establishments.
observations.managementAction:
Strict enforcement of Section 48 of the General Provisions of the GAA of 2015.

Require all employees to get clearance from Municipal Accountant and HRMO before obtaining loans from government and private financial institutions.
observations.reason: Some personnel asked for human consideration to allow them to apply for a loan despite the favor would result to a   below the mandatory  net take home pay of P3,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC to be resolute of its responsibility in ensuring that the LGU abides by the standards set forth in the government procurement law.  It may create an internal control unit to make sure all provisions of RA 9184 are adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Direct BAC secretariat to strictly adhere provisions of RA 9184 and to attach proof of PHILGEPS publication on vouchers.
observations.reason:
Not all procuring notices as required by pertinent procurement regulations were posted. 

Reiterated, observation no. 4.D.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the management to:

Revisit, review and evaluate the programs/projects/activities included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund; and 

Ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity-related activities so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Confer with the MDRRMC to revisit, review and evaluate Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for implementation of LDRRFMP.
observations.reason: Some disbursements in 2016 were still not in the purview of expenditures as mentioned in the pertinent regulation.

observations.managementAction:
Direct Municipal Accountant to assign personnel in-charge to review supporting documents to the claims for timely submission to COA.

Established coordinative mechanism between the Office of the Municipal Accountant and Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effective document tracking system and transmittal to ensure prompt preparation and submission of documents and reports.
observations.reason: See reason cited in observation no. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents attached to the remittances as provided in BIR Rules and Regulations and require Authorization Letter from the concerned Revenue District Officer (RDO) authorizing the temporary issuance of the manual receipts of the Collecting Officer and ensure that eReceipt shall be received upon the availability of the Collection Officer Receipting Device (CORD) or follow up from the concerned BIR Collector the issuance of eReceipt to ensure that remittances are properly accounted for in the books of the BIR.
observations.managementAction:
Assigned personnel to look into the completeness of supporting documents attached to the BIR remittances.

Make presentation with BIR for possible issuance of temporary manual/receipt pending issuance of eReceipt.

Secured PNDF as the basis for the procurement of drugs and medicines.

Conferred to lay down policies to properly ascertain the propriety of accounts used in appropriating and accounting of drugs and medicines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct yearly physical inventory of the agency’s properties.  Require the Accountant and Treasurer to maintain and reconcile the ledger cards and property cards and issue Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) every time there is a transfer of equipment from one accountable officer to another to prevent future problems in pinpointing accountability and responsibility on the issued equipment and to avoid abuse or misuse of the same.
observations.managementAction: Creation of team to conduct inventory of the agency’s properties.
observations.reason:
See reason cited in observation no. 9.

Reiterated, in Part II, observation no. 7.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to create an Ad-Hoc Committee who will be responsible in updating and reconciling the ledger balances with the treasurer’s cashbook and bank statements for all funds.  Also, require the chief of Treasury and Accounting offices to improve/strengthen the existing internal controls over custody and inter-office routing of transactions, to ensure timely recording in the respective books.
observations.managementAction:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to assign personnel/s to perform the reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer’s Financial records and to designate personnel in-charge of preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.

Issuance of office order of the personnel/s to perform the reconciliation and the personnel in-charge of preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: Only Bank Reconciliation Statement for General Fund was made and none for the SEF and Trust Fund accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to submit promptly, the required reports and supporting documents for the immediate examination/audit/review/ reconciliation of COA, as follows: 

Liquidation of cash advance (by the accountable officer)

Salaries and wages – within 5 days after each 15th day/end of the month pay period.
Petty operating expenses and field operating expenses – within 20 days after the end of the year; subject to replenishments during the year

Report of Disburse-ments / Checks Issued / Collections

Within 10 days after receipt from the disbursing officer of  said reports with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents.
Report of Collections
Within 10 days after the documents were received and recorded in the books of accounts.

Financial reports 

 Not later than the 10th day of the following month.
Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

observations.recommendation:
Notify immediately the auditor of the occurrence of the loss and submit within 30 days the following documents for evaluation and determination if relief maybe warranted (Section 73, P.D. 1445): 

Affidavit executed by the accountable officer stating the following facts:

Property lost and its valuation
Actual date of occurrence
Other evidences/ facts, diligence exercised 

2.  Final Police Report 

3.  Comments and/ or recommendation of the agency head

4. Comments and/or recommendation of the auditor as a result of the investigation and evaluation of the causes of the loss and the evidences submitted.

5.  Certification from Fire Chief as to the destruction

6.  Inspection Report on the extent of damage

7.  Affidavit of two (2) disinterested persons

observations.recommendation: Withhold the amount equivalent to ten percent (10%) of every progress billing, before payment is made to the contractor.  This would guarantee for uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities, if any.

observations.recommendation: Adopt and implement corrective measures to ensure that trust liabilities are remitted on time and that sufficient cash is available to meet all obligations as they fall due, pursuant to Sections 305(e) and 337 of RA 7160 and Section 4(3) of PD 1445. Stop the practice of incurring obligations without sufficient cash availability.
observations.managementAction: Strict implementation of cash allotment system.

observations.recommendation: Provide/furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader with copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate the inspection of the projects, pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Management to:

Exercise due diligence in the preparation and processing of claims for payment. All disbursements should be supported with complete documentation to support the validity and correctness of the claims for payment.

Ensure the legality of transactions and conformity with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Direct Municipal Accountant to assign personnel in-charge to perform pre-audit to ensure completeness of documents prior to approval of the Municipal Accountant.

Issuance of office order of the personnel in-charge to perform pre-audit.

Various claims against government funds were disbursed without sufficient documentation.

See related observation in Part II, observation no.4.A.
